event: The Marriage of Alexander and Roxanne
symbols: wedding chamber — the union of East and West through marriage
putti playing with armor — love disarming the warrior
veiled bride — modesty and the unveiling of beauty
description: A large Renaissance fresco within an elaborate decorative border showing the wedding scene of Alexander the Great and Roxanne. Alexander in armor and a red cloak approaches Roxanne, who sits on a bed or couch at left, partially veiled. Numerous nude putti (cupids) frolic around them — some playing with Alexander's armor, others flying above. The architectural setting features classical columns and a richly decorated interior. The composition is framed by an ornate painted border with figures, garlands, and architectural elements.

_meta.title: The Marriage of Alexander and Roxanne
